title: Girlfriend of Steel
wikitext:
[]: Girlfriend of Steel?             Quote Post by slothen » Thu Apr 05, 2007 11:07 am Its a pc video game, right? So how do I get it? that is to say, how do I get a version accessible to us moonspeak-impaired?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Apr 05, 2007 11:41 am GoS1: Fantranslation GoS2: Raw Japanese Reichu-megami-sama has kindly written a walkthrough, may it guide you on your quest. Both are "dating sims" although the latter makes good of GAINAX's promise of fanservice and takes place in the Possible World of episode 26 Yes, Shinji's something of an ecchi in this world just as the scene in episode 26 shows* -_- . "Panties!" "Giggle" "Panties!" "LOL" and so on and so forth. Has one of two endings you can choose at the end, one where you go with Asuka, another where you end up with Rei. The former takes place during the actual series, IMHO after episode 15 (because of Asuka's blatant flirting with Shinji and mentioning something about kissing him), when a transfer student named Kirishima Mana shows up and instantly admits to liking Shinji. And hi-jinks of stopping a Robot that Mana is possibly involved with ensue as a wacky love triangle between Asuka/Shinji/Mana also ensues. Has one of three endings you can choose at the end. One that involves Asuka, one that deals with Mana, and another that isn't character specific is it includes Kaji, Misato, Shinji, Asuka and Mana. Although the problem of both these "games" is their mostly long flash animations, not much interactivity (though I personally enjoyed them and I think according to Reichu's page, games like this are common in Japan?) In terms of where to get it, I suggest the magic of bittorrent. If you want me to point you where exactly site/page-wise, PM me so I can let you know. ;) *Part of the reason I like the GoS2 manga "Angelic Days". Shinji seems more or less just like his normal anime counterpart. Oh yeah, and both games are done by the seiyuu from the series, Mana's voice being done by Megumi Hayashibara. Wow, I rambled a lot, didn't I? ^^;

[]: Re: Girlfriend of Steel?             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Apr 05, 2007 11:51 am slothen wrote: Its a pc video game, right? So how do I get it? that is to say, how do I get a version accessible to us moonspeak-impaired?

[]:             Quote Post by Executor » Sat Apr 07, 2007 10:36 pm *Girlfriend of Steel? *insert peddantic joke about coming :P* ANd now I claim this thread in the name of the Empire!

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Sat Apr 07, 2007 11:34 pm Executor wrote: *Girlfriend of Steel? *insert peddantic joke about coming :P* ANd now I claim this thread in the name of the Empire!

[]:             Quote Post by Executor » Sun Apr 08, 2007 12:19 am slothen wrote: Executor wrote: *Girlfriend of Steel? *insert peddantic joke about coming :P* ANd now I claim this thread in the name of the Empire!

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Sun Apr 08, 2007 12:30 am Girlfriend of Steel = Awesome Mana Kirishima = Awesome If you haven't played this game (which is not much of a game, but more like another episode of the show) yet, then you need to do it asap.

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Sun Apr 08, 2007 12:42 am i know.. im working on it.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Apr 08, 2007 12:44 am Executor wrote: ... You would know this how? Besides, it was a joke anyways.

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Sun Apr 08, 2007 12:47 am I had to urban dictionary that. For the Fail? I've always heard and used "For the Loss." Loss is the opposite of win anyway. I have yet to engage in mana bashing, although from what I've seen heard of her, I suspect I'll like the character. Yeah, no one's seeding it right now i guess. two days and im at 7.2 percent. :? I mean, 3kbps dl from peers FTL

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Apr 08, 2007 1:00 am slothen wrote: I had to urban dictionary that. For the Fail? I've always heard and used "For the Loss." Loss is the opposite of win anyway.

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Sun Apr 08, 2007 1:19 am btw Reichu you use the word "Meme" so much I was forced to look it up. You say it like its a bad thing. FTF is in the urban dictionary as for the fail in the fifth entry. Is that "please don't like her?" Even if I do, I don't expect myself to lobby to get her in webuild or ELAM or anything. I already think the premise of GoS is pretty dumb. Ya, I use a shitty program, but its adequate, and small. Beyond that I don't see why I should care.

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Mon Apr 09, 2007 4:07 pm is it possible to lower the volume of background music?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Apr 09, 2007 4:29 pm With GoS1, in terms of the midis if you lower "Sw synth" (if memory serves me right) then it's quieter. GoS2, I don't remember but since it's mp3 format (though the looping's on the "meh" side), your best bet is just lowering the volume in general.

[]:             Quote Post by The Bastard King » Tue Apr 10, 2007 1:58 am T <bleep> ts? What the hell's a t <bleep> t? Oh, wait, torrent. Right.

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Tue Apr 10, 2007 4:27 am The Bastard King wrote: T <bleep> ts? What the hell's a t <bleep> t? Oh, wait, torrent. Right.

[]:             Quote Post by Katayoku no Tenshi » Tue Apr 10, 2007 5:34 am The Bastard King wrote: T<bleep>ts? What the hell's a t<bleep>t? Oh, wait, torrent. Right.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Apr 10, 2007 9:09 am Nice going, you Bastard King . My sound card lets me adjust MIDI synth separately from everything else. I can just mute it out and leave all other audio "intact".

[]:             Quote Post by The Bastard King » Tue Apr 10, 2007 11:51 am Well, now I feel estranged and outcast. F <bleep> k.

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Tue Apr 10, 2007 1:36 pm ya that should work, I forgot. This game is about 10 times as long as i thought it would be.
